title: Phrynium micans
taxonomy: Phrynium micans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. It belongs to the genus Phrynium, which is a group of plants commonly known as prayer plants or Phrynium plants. This genus is part of the order Zingiberales, a group of flowering plants that also includes ginger and bananas. The Marantaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical regions of the world. Phrynium micans is native to tropical Africa.

intro: Phrynium micans, also known as the shining club fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Phrynium micans include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
